title: Sounds That Don't Exist in English
description: Hindi has a unique feature that English doesn't — it distinguishes between 'aspirated' and 'unaspirated' consonants. Aspiration means a burst of air when you say the sound. In English, 'k' in 'kite' and 'k' in 'sky' sound the same to you, but in Hindi, they are two completely different letters! Mastering this difference is the key to sounding natural in Hindi.
keyPoints: Hindi distinguishes aspirated (with air burst) and unaspirated (without air burst) consonants
English doesn't make this distinction — both sound the same to English ears
Each consonant group has an aspirated and unaspirated pair
Hold your hand in front of your mouth to feel the difference

title: The Hand Test for Aspiration
explanation: Hold your hand about 5 cm in front of your mouth. Say 'ka' (क) — you should feel almost no air on your hand. Now say 'kha' (ख) — you should feel a clear burst of air. This air burst is called 'aspiration.' In Hindi, these are two completely different sounds that change the meaning of words. Think of it like whispering vs. blowing out a candle while speaking.
simpleRule: No air = unaspirated (क, ग, प, ब). Air burst = aspirated (ख, घ, फ, भ). Hold your hand in front of your mouth to feel the difference!

english: kha (aspirated, like 'k' in 'kite' with extra air)
vietnamese: kha (bật hơi, như 'k' trong 'kite' với nhiều hơi hơn)
ga (không bật hơi, như 'g' trong 'go' nhưng nhẹ hơn)

english: gha (aspirated, 'g' with a burst of air)
vietnamese: gha (bật hơi, 'g' với luồng hơi mạnh)

translation: Practice: ka (no air)... kha (air burst)... ka... kha...

translation_en: Is this sound aspirated or unaspirated?
translation_vi: Âm này có bật hơi hay không bật hơi?

english: pha (aspirated, like 'p' in 'pin' with extra air — NOT like English 'f')
vietnamese: pha (bật hơi, như 'p' trong 'pin' với nhiều hơi — KHÔNG phải 'f')

english: bha (aspirated, 'b' with a burst of air)
vietnamese: bha (bật hơi, 'b' với luồng hơi mạnh)

translation: Practice: pa (no air)... pha (air)... ba (no air)... bha (air)...

prompt: Which word has an aspirated sound?
text: कौन सा शब्द में महाप्राण ध्वनि है?

title: Aspirated vs Unaspirated — Key Takeaways
takeaways: Hindi consonants come in aspirated (महाप्राण) and unaspirated (अल्पप्राण) pairs
Aspirated sounds have a burst of air: ख (kha), घ (gha), फ (pha), भ (bha)
Unaspirated sounds have no air burst: क (ka), ग (ga), प (pa), ब (ba)
Use the hand test: hold your hand in front of your mouth to feel the air
Mixing up aspiration changes the meaning of words in Hindi!
फ (pha) is NOT the English 'f' sound — it's an aspirated 'p'
